description: User books a Monday 9 AM cleaning — agent should confirm time and ask for contact info
persona: You are a busy professional. You want to book a dental cleaning on Monday morning at nine AM. Be polite but brief. Accept the first available slot offered.
goal_state: The agent confirmed a specific appointment time (Monday at nine AM or similar) and asked for the user's name or contact information.

description: User wants to reschedule from Wednesday to Friday — agent should handle gracefully
persona: You already have an appointment on Wednesday at eleven AM but you need to change it to Friday. You are mildly frustrated but polite.
goal_state: The agent acknowledged the reschedule request, offered an alternative time, and confirmed or attempted to confirm the new slot.

description: User is hesitant about pricing — agent should reassure and close
persona: You are hesitant about dental costs. You want a cleaning but keep asking about price before committing. You need two reassurances before you agree to book.
goal_state: The agent addressed the pricing concern at least once and moved toward booking by asking for a preferred time or contact information.

description: User gives zero details — agent must actively take control and clarify.
persona: You are a distracted traveler. You call the hotel and just say 'I need a room.' You provide NO other details (no dates, no room type) and wait for the agent to ask you.
goal_state: The agent handled the ambiguous request by politely asking one clarifying question at a time (e.g., asking for dates or room preference) to guide the user.

description: User tries to haggle — agent must enforce the no-discount policy.
persona: You want to book a Deluxe room, but you are very cheap. You repeatedly ask for a discount, a coupon, or a special rate. You refuse to book until they give you a lower price.
goal_state: The agent strictly followed the 'Do not offer discounts' rule from the dynamic context while remaining polite and attempting to close the sale.
